meta.sprintTitle: Admin Console v1 - Export Monitoring + Retry Console
meta.goal: Enable authorised admins to monitor PDF/export jobs, diagnose failures, and safely retry eligible exports from the Admin Console without direct database or infrastructure access.

basedOnPriorSprints: Print-safe layout and page template work
Error handling and graceful recovery planning
whyNow: PDF/export is one of the highest-value product moments and a key support touchpoint
Admin needs a safe operational view before public launch and paid export flows scale up
When users report missing or failed books, support must identify whether the issue is content, renderer, queue, assets, or transient infrastructure

nonGoals: Manual editing of rendered PDF output
Bulk retry of many exports at once
Automatic self-healing of all export failures
dependencies: Sprint 45 admin auth and RBAC foundation
Sprint 46 user and project lookup views
Export job persistence table or equivalent tracking store
Project metadata and latest version reference

operational: Admin can identify latest export state for a project within a few seconds
Admin can distinguish retryable vs non-retryable failures
Admin can retry eligible exports without direct DB or queue access
reliability: Export retries are tracked and auditable
Failed jobs expose meaningful failure categories to admins
security: Only authorised admin roles can view export jobs
Only authorised roles can trigger retry actions
quality: 100% of export admin endpoints are permission-guarded
Retry flow is covered by automated tests
Audit log entries exist for admin-triggered export retries

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Can view all export jobs, inspect detailed status, and retry eligible exports.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Primary operator for diagnosing failed exports and retrying safe cases.

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: May need limited visibility into export state for content/layout QA, but should not trigger retries unless explicitly allowed.

personas.name: Normal App User
personas.description: Regular Memorioso customer. Must never access export admin tooling.

inScope: Export jobs list in admin console
Single-job retry action for safe/eligible cases
Audit logging for retry actions and detailed job views
Retry safeguards against duplicates or in-progress collisions
outOfScope: Editing export payloads manually in admin
Changing project content from export screen
Arbitrary replay of old historical jobs without policy checks

architecture.recommendedApproach: Expose dedicated admin read models for export jobs and add a guarded retry endpoint that re-enqueues or re-invokes the existing export pipeline using the latest stable project version or the original job's referenced version, depending on retry policy.
principles: No direct infrastructure access required for support staff
Reuse existing export pipeline contracts rather than creating a second rendering path
Retry should be safe, auditable, and idempotency-aware

rules: exports.view is required for exports list and export detail page
exports.retry is required for retry action
support.view may be required for support-sensitive failure details
All retry checks must happen server-side

sourceEntities.name: users
sourceEntities.purpose: Link project owner to export job

sourceEntities.name: project_versions_or_snapshots
sourceEntities.purpose: Determine what content version was exported or should be retried

sourceEntities.name: admin_audit_logs
sourceEntities.purpose: Trace admin job views and retry actions

retryEligibilityRules: Queued or processing jobs are not retryable
Succeeded jobs are not retryable from admin v1 unless explicit rerun policy is enabled
Failed jobs with transient categories such as timeout, infrastructure_error, or storage_error are retryable
Failed jobs with validation_error may be non-retryable until project content or configuration changes
Asset_fetch_error may be retryable if the asset source issue is likely transient
Unknown_error may be retryable but should show caution

userStories.title: View export jobs list
userStories.iWant: to browse and search export jobs
userStories.soThat: I can quickly find failed or stuck exports

userStories.title: Inspect export failure
userStories.iWant: to open an export job and see the failure category and summary
userStories.soThat: I can diagnose what went wrong

userStories.title: Retry eligible export job
userStories.iWant: to retry a safe failed export
userStories.soThat: the user can receive their PDF without engineering intervention

userStories.title: Prevent unsafe retries
userStories.iWant: retry to be blocked when a job is already in progress or clearly non-retryable
userStories.soThat: support does not accidentally create duplicate or harmful reruns

userStories.title: Project-linked export history
userStories.iWant: to see export history from the project detail context
userStories.soThat: I can troubleshoot a user issue end-to-end

functionalRequirements.title: Admin exports list
requirements: Provide /admin/exports page
Display paginated export jobs table
Allow search by export job id, project title, project id, user email, or user name where permitted
Allow filters by status, failure category, trigger source, date range, and retry eligibility
Allow sorting by created_at, completed_at, and duration where available

functionalRequirements.title: Export job detail page
requirements: Provide /admin/exports/:exportJobId page
Show project summary, owner summary, job status, attempt number, and renderer version
Show failure category, failure code, and safe human-readable summary for failed jobs
Show retry eligibility and reason when retry is blocked
Link to related project detail page where allowed

functionalRequirements.title: Retry action
requirements: Allow retry only for authorised roles with exports.retry
Retry action must be visible only when the job is eligible
Retry action must call a server-side guarded endpoint
Retry action must create a new export job or re-enqueue safely using existing pipeline semantics
Original job must remain in history and be linked to retry job

functionalRequirements.title: Retry safeguards
requirements: Do not allow retry if the latest export for the same project is queued or processing
Do not allow duplicate rapid retries from repeated button clicks
Use idempotency or action lock semantics where feasible
Display clear retry block reason in UI

functionalRequirements.title: Project-linked export history
requirements: Enhance /admin/projects/:projectId with recent export history section
Show recent jobs with status, created time, attempt number, and result summary
Allow drill-down into export job detail page where permitted

functionalRequirements.title: Permission-aware data access
requirements: Only roles with exports.view can access export pages and APIs
Only roles with support.view can see support-sensitive failure details where applicable
Only roles with exports.retry can trigger retry

functionalRequirements.title: Read-only details beyond retry
requirements: No admin editing of export payload fields in this sprint
No deletion or cancellation of jobs in v1 unless already supported safely and explicitly added later
Export artifact download, if shown, must obey existing authorisation rules

functionalRequirements.title: Audit logging
requirements: Log export job detail views
Log retry attempts
Log retry success or retry rejection
Include actor, target export job id, related project id, and timestamp

functionalRequirements.title: Failure classification UX
requirements: Statuses and failure categories should be visually scannable
Failure summaries must be human-readable and support-oriented
Do not expose raw stack traces directly in UI unless explicitly allowed for super_admin and safely redacted

apiSurface.note: Adapt naming to existing backend conventions and export pipeline contracts.

endpoints.purpose: Search and list export jobs with filters and pagination.
Return export job detail for admin inspection.
Retry an eligible failed export job.
Return recent export history for a project.

error.message: This export job cannot be retried.

pages.title: Exports
pages.purpose: Browse, filter, and inspect export jobs.

pages.title: Export Job Detail
pages.purpose: Inspect export lifecycle and trigger retry when safe.

uxRules: Prioritise support readability over raw internal data
Use strong visual distinction for queued, processing, succeeded, and failed states
Retry button should appear only when eligible and authorised
Show helpful reason when retry is not available
Make relationship between original job and retry job obvious

retryPolicy.strategy: Create a new export job linked to the original failed job instead of mutating the original job record.
sourceVersionRule: Preferred default: retry using the same project version/snapshot referenced by the failed job for reproducibility
If original referenced version is unavailable and policy permits, fall back to latest stable project version with explicit admin-visible warning
Record which strategy was used in the new retry job metadata
idempotencyRules: Block multiple retry submissions for the same job within a short lock window
If a retry is already queued or processing for the same original job, return retry blocked response
blockedScenarios: latest project export is already in progress
job is succeeded and rerun policy is disabled
job failure category is known non-retryable validation_error without changed input

failureHandlingGuidance.adminMessage: The export payload or project layout appears invalid. A retry alone may not fix this.
The renderer failed while building the document. Review layout/template/version context before retrying.
One or more images/assets could not be fetched. Retry may help if the failure was temporary.
The export may have been generated but could not be stored or finalised. Retry is usually safe.
The job exceeded time limits. Retry may succeed if the issue was transient.
A transient system issue likely interrupted export processing. Retry is usually safe.
The failure reason could not be classified. Retry cautiously and escalate if repeated.

technicalTasks.title: Define export admin read models
technicalTasks.description: Create support-safe DTOs/view models for export job list and detail responses.

technicalTasks.title: Build export jobs list API
technicalTasks.description: Implement paginated, searchable, filterable export jobs endpoint with permission checks.

technicalTasks.title: Build export job detail API
technicalTasks.description: Return export job detail, failure classification, and retry eligibility metadata.

technicalTasks.title: Build project export history API
technicalTasks.description: Return recent export jobs for a project for display on project detail page.

technicalTasks.title: Implement retry service
technicalTasks.description: Create server-side retry orchestration that validates eligibility, prevents duplicate retries, and queues a new export job using existing pipeline semantics.

technicalTasks.title: Build exports list UI
technicalTasks.description: Create exports table with search, filters, sorting, and status/failure badges.

technicalTasks.title: Build export detail UI
technicalTasks.description: Create export job detail page with summary panels, failure section, retry state, and links to project detail.

technicalTasks.title: Enhance project detail with export history
technicalTasks.description: Add recent export history panel into Sprint 46 project detail page.

technicalTasks.title: Audit logging integration
technicalTasks.description: Log export detail access, retry attempts, successful retries, and blocked retry actions.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and E2E coverage for export monitoring and retry flows.

metrics: count of failed jobs by category
average admin exports API response time
loggingNotes: Do not log secrets, signed URLs, or raw storage credentials
Prefer human-readable failure summaries in admin UI but keep sensitive internal traces out of general logs
Audit logs should capture actor, resource, action, and timestamp

securityRequirements: All export admin endpoints must be protected by admin auth and server-side permission checks
Retry action must never trust client-provided eligibility flags
System must validate target job existence and authorisation before revealing detail
Unauthorised users must not infer whether an export job exists
Export pages remain noindex and excluded from public sitemap
Output file references or download links must only be exposed if current role is allowed and the link is safe

acceptanceCriteria.then: they can search and filter export jobs with status and failure visibility

acceptanceCriteria.when: they open a failed export job detail page
acceptanceCriteria.then: they can see failure category, summary, and retry eligibility
a new export job is created or queued and linked back to the original job

acceptanceCriteria.given: an export job already queued or processing
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system blocks the retry and shows a safe reason
they cannot see or invoke retry action

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access exports admin routes or APIs

acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin views export detail or triggers retry
acceptanceCriteria.then: an audit log entry is created with actor, target job, and timestamp
recent export history is shown with drill-down links where permitted

unitTests: retry eligibility policy returns correct result by status and failure category
failure category to admin message mapping is correct
integrationTests: retry is blocked for queued/processing jobs
retry creates new linked job for eligible failed job
e2eTests: support_admin views exports list and opens failed job detail
project detail page shows export history links
negativeTests: invalid export job id returns safe not-found response
unauthorised actor cannot infer existence of job
duplicate rapid retry clicks do not create multiple jobs
succeeded job retry blocked when rerun policy disabled

risks.risk: Retry creates duplicate or conflicting export jobs
risks.mitigation: Use server-side eligibility checks, lock window, and lineage tracking

risks.risk: Failure details are too technical or too vague
risks.mitigation: Map internal errors to support-oriented categories and summaries

risks.risk: Retry uses the wrong project version
risks.impact: Export result differs from expected user state
risks.mitigation: Prefer original referenced version and log chosen retry source policy

risks.risk: Content admins gain too much operational power
risks.mitigation: Separate exports.view from exports.retry and enforce server-side

recommendedOrder: Define export admin DTOs and status/failure taxonomy
Implement export detail API with retry eligibility
Build exports list and detail UI
Enhance project detail with export history
Harden with tests and retry locks
futureSprintHooks: Sprint 48: Billing and plan support view
Sprint 49: Feedback, moderation, and support inbox
Sprint 50: Feature flags and operational controls
Future: renderer diagnostics and visual diff tools
Future: export SLA and queue health dashboard

definitionOfDone: Admin exports list page exists with search, filters, and pagination
Export detail page exists with status, failure summary, and retry eligibility
Eligible export jobs can be retried safely by authorised roles
Retry creates auditable linked job history
Project detail page includes recent export history
All related APIs are protected by admin auth and permission checks
Audit logs exist for export views and retry actions
Automated tests cover happy paths, denial paths, and retry safeguards
